parts.title: Part 1
parts.content:
Questions 1-10

Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Alex’s Training Example Alex complete his training in ….. 2014 …… About the applicant: At first, Alex did his training in the [1] department. Alex didn’t have a qualification from school in [2] . Alex thinks he should have done the diploma in [3] skills. Age of other trainees: the youngest was [4] . Benefits of doing training at JPNW: Lots of opportunities because of the size of the organisation. Trainees receive the same amount of [5] as permanent staff. The training experience increases people’s confidence a lot. Trainees go to [6] one day per month. The company is in a convenient [7] . Advice for interview: Don’t wear [8] . Don’t be [9] . Make sure you [10] .

parts.title: Part 2
parts.content:
Questions 11-16

Choose the correct letter, A , B or C . The Snow Centre

Questions 17-20

What information does Annie give about skiing on each of the following mountain trails? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F , next to Questions Information Mountain trails

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 11-16

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

The Snow Centre

contentBlocks.prompt: Annie recommends that when cross-country skiing, the visitors should
options: A. get away from the regular trails.
B. stop to enjoy views of the scenery.
C. go at a slow speed at the beginning.

contentBlocks.prompt: What does Annie tell the group about this afternoon’s dog-sled trip?
options: A. Those who want to can take part in a race.
B. Anyone has the chance to drive a team of dogs.
C. One group member will be chosen to lead the trail.

contentBlocks.prompt: What does Annie say about the team relay event?
options: A. All participants receive a medal.
B. The course is 4 km long.
C. Each team is led by a teacher.

contentBlocks.prompt: On the snow-shoe trip, the visitors will
options: A. visit an old gold mine.
B. learn about unusual flowers.
C. climb to the top of a mountain.

contentBlocks.prompt: The cost of accommodation in the mountain hut includes
options: A. a supply of drinking water.
B. transport of visitors’ luggage.
C. cooked meals.

contentBlocks.prompt: If there is a storm while the visitors are in the hut, they should
options: A. contact the bus driver.
B. wait until the weather improves.
C. use the emergency locator beacon.

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 17-20

What information does Annie give about skiing on each of the following mountain trails?

Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions

Information

Mountain trails

options: A. It has a good place to stop and rest.
B. It is suitable for all abilities.
C. It involves crossing a river.
D. It demands a lot of skill.
E. It may be closed in bad weather.
F. It has some very narrow sections.

parts.title: Part 3
parts.content:
Questions 21-26

Choose the correct letter, A , B or C . Labels giving nutritional information on food packaging

Questions 27-28

Choose TWO letters, A-E .

Questions 29-30

Choose TWO letters, A-E .

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 21-26

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Labels giving nutritional information on food packaging

contentBlocks.prompt: What was Jack’s attitude to nutritional food labels before this project?
options: A. He didn’t read everything on them.
B. He didn’t think they were important.
C. He thought they were too complicated.

contentBlocks.prompt: Alice says that before doing this project,
options: A. she was unaware of what certain foods contained.
B. she was too lazy to read food labels.
C. she was only interested in the number of calories.

contentBlocks.prompt: When discussing supermarket brands of pizza, Jack agrees with Alice that
options: A. the list of ingredients is shocking.
B. he will hesitate before buying pizza again.
C. the nutritional label is misleading.

contentBlocks.prompt: Jack prefers the daily value system to other labelling systems because it is
options: A. more accessible.
B. more logical.
C. more comprehensive.

contentBlocks.prompt: What surprised both students about one flavour of crisps?
options: A. The percentage of artificial additives given was incorrect.
B. The products did not contain any meat.
C. The labels did not list all the ingredients.

contentBlocks.prompt: What do the students think about research into the impact of nutritional food labelling?
options: A. It did not produce clear results.
B. It focused on the wrong people.
C. It made unrealistic recommendations.

contentBlocks.prompt: Which TWO things surprised the students about the traffic-light system for nutritional labels?
options: A. its widespread use
B. the fact that it is voluntary for supermarkets
C. how little research was done before its introduction
D. its unpopularity with food manufacturers
E. the way that certain colours are used

contentBlocks.prompt: Which TWO things are true about the participants in the study on the traffic-light system?
options: A. They had low literacy levels.
B. They were regular consumers of packaged food.
C. They were selected randomly.
D. They were from all socio-economic groups.
E. They were interviewed face-to-face.

parts.title: Part 4
parts.content:
Questions 31-40

Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. The history of coffee Coffee in the Arab world These was small-scale trade in wild coffee from Ethiopia. 1522: Coffee was approved in the Ottoman court as a type of medicine. 1623: In Constantinople, the ruler ordered the [31] of every coffee house. Coffee arrives in Europe (17th century) Coffee shops were compared to [32] . They played an important part in social and [33] changes. Coffee and European colonization European powers established coffee plantations in their colonies. Types of coffee were often named according to the [34] they came from. In Brazil and the Caribbean, most cultivation depended on [35] . In Java, coffee was used as a form of [36] . Coffee became almost as important as [37] . The move towards the consumption of [38] in Britain did not also take place in the USA. Coffee in the 19th century Prices dropped because of improvements in [39] . Industrial workers found coffee helped them to work at [40] .
